summary: Gruber analyzes a WSJ report claiming Apple ordered 5-6 million Apple Watches for its initial production run, with roughly half being Sport models, one-third standard, and the remainder gold Edition units. He suspects these numbers leaked from supply chain sources rather than Apple executives. The most striking implication is financial: if Apple sells 1 million gold Edition units per quarter at $5,000-$10,000 each, the Edition alone could generate $5-10 billion quarterly. This would mean the gold Edition dominates Apple Watch revenue despite being a minority of unit sales, mirroring how Apple captures majority profits with minority market share across iPhones, iPads, and Macs.
key_points: Apple's initial Apple Watch production order was reportedly 5-6 million units split roughly 50% Sport, 33% standard, 17% Edition
The gold Edition's revenue could dwarf all other Apple Watch models combined, potentially generating $5-10 billion per quarter
It is unprecedented in the watch industry for a luxury model to be assembled in China, but scale may require it
The Edition would replicate Apple's broader business pattern: capturing the majority of profits with a minority of unit sales
Gruber signals skepticism about the WSJ's supply chain sources and notes Apple had no strategic reason to leak these numbers
money_quotes: I would wager — heavily — that these numbers come from supply chain sources, not Apple executives.
I believe it is unprecedented in the watch industry for a luxury model to be assembled in China.
If Apple actually sells 1 million Edition units per quarter, and they sell for an ASP of $5,000, that's $5 billion in revenue per quarter — just for the gold Edition models.
3 million Sport units at $350 comes to "only" $1 billion or so.

The Edition models would thus do to the Apple Watch lineup as a whole what the iPhone, iPad, and Macintosh do to the entire phone, tablet, and PC industries, respectively: achieve a decided majority of the profits with a decided minority of the unit sales.
When I was a freshman at Drexel in 1991, there was a kid in my dorm with a $12,000 Mac IIfx. (He was an asshole who cheated at Spectre.)
key_insight: If the reported production numbers are accurate, the gold Apple Watch Edition would dominate the product line's revenue despite minimal unit share, extending Apple's proven business model of capturing outsized profits from premium products.
